jokes.text: You're my half-brother, but there's no law against killing the other half!
jokes.setup: Typical soap opera family drama with half-siblings
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of 'half-brother' as justification for murder
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'half-brother' with dark humor. Good structure and economy but not deeply character-specific.

jokes.text: Slurm. It's addictive!
jokes.punchline: Honest advertising admitting the product is addictive
jokes.explanation: Subverts advertising conventions with brutal honesty. Great economy and originality.

jokes.text: Whammy-wham-wham-wazzle. Let's party!
jokes.punchline: Nonsense catchphrase that sounds like party slang
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the character's over-the-top party persona with memorable nonsense phrase.

jokes.text: Who says there are no more heroes?
jokes.punchline: Calling a party worm drinking Slurm a hero
jokes.explanation: Shows Fry's naive enthusiasm and misplaced priorities effectively.

jokes.text: Odds are mathematically insignificant. / I like those odds.
jokes.punchline: Fry likes odds specifically because they're terrible
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Fry's backwards logic and optimism despite facts.

jokes.text: Rats! Just another tooth.
jokes.setup: Looking for golden bottle cap prize
jokes.punchline: Finding a human tooth instead, treating it as mundane
jokes.explanation: Dark implication about Slurm contents delivered casually. Good visual absurdity.

jokes.text: Right up until my third heart attack.
jokes.setup: Fry comparing his past cola addiction
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of multiple heart attacks
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and economy. Dark humor delivered matter-of-factly fits Fry's character.

jokes.text: Let me check if you have a fever. Ow!
jokes.setup: Leela checking robot's temperature like a human
jokes.punchline: Burns her hand on hot robot
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy. Functional but predictable - treating robot like human setup.

jokes.text: he's running a fever of 900 degrees
jokes.punchline: Impossibly high temperature delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation of the fever concept for a robot.

jokes.text: I'm not Bender. I'm Fry. / I thought you were the robot. / Nope. Human.
jokes.punchline: Doctor confused about who is the robot vs human
jokes.explanation: Shows Zoidberg's incompetence effectively but the confusion is fairly basic.

jokes.text: I couldn't tell him. It's fin fungus. He'll be floating upside down by morning.
jokes.setup: Doctor giving reassuring diagnosis to patient
jokes.punchline: Privately admitting patient will die horribly
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Zoidberg's terrible bedside manner and incompetence with dark humor.

jokes.text: I'm 40% zinc. / Then Echinacea or St. John's wort. / Or a big fat placebo. It's all the same crap.
jokes.punchline: Bender dismissing all alternative medicine as equally worthless
jokes.explanation: Sharp writing that's both scientifically accurate and perfectly fits Bender's cynical personality.

jokes.text: I call this the F-ray. It's an x-ray that lets you see through anything, even metal.
jokes.punchline: F-ray name and capability that makes x-rays obsolete
jokes.explanation: Decent sci-fi parody invention name with logical escalation beyond x-rays.

jokes.text: The neutrino beam it emits is dangerous... so you'll all need protective goggles. / Huh?
jokes.setup: Professor warning about dangerous radiation requiring protection
jokes.punchline: Goggles as inadequate protection against neutrino beam
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist logic - protective goggles against neutrino radiation. Shows Professor's absent-minded approach to safety.

jokes.text: You may feel a slight stinging sensation. All of you.
jokes.setup: Warning about side effects of medical procedure
jokes.punchline: Admitting everyone will be affected, not just the patient
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and understatement. Shows Professor's casual disregard for everyone's safety.

jokes.text: That's my watch. / I was wondering where I put that.
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of theft presented as absent-mindedness
jokes.explanation: Shows Fry's obliviousness to social norms but the setup is predictable.

jokes.text: And for the love of God, don't let it fall into the wrong hands.
jokes.setup: Serious warning about dangerous device security
jokes.punchline: Immediately followed by crew using it irresponsibly
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for immediate subversion. Good structure but somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: Ow! My sperm! / Neat. Mind if I try that again?
jokes.punchline: Bender wanting to repeat harmful action
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment showing his sadistic enjoyment of others' pain. Good economy.

jokes.text: Didn't hurt that time.
jokes.punchline: Implying first exposure destroyed fertility
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect economy. The implication is funnier than explicit explanation.

jokes.text: That's no lady! / One more upgrade and I'll be more lady than you can handle.
jokes.setup: Revealing Bender's male identity under feminine appearance
jokes.punchline: Bender's confident response about his transition progress
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Bender's vanity and confidence. Good callback to his gender-bending episodes.

jokes.text: Bite my shiny, metal ass. / You couldn't afford it, honey.
jokes.punchline: Amy treating it as a paid service offer
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of Bender's signature line. Amy's comeback is perfect - treating insult as business proposition.

jokes.text: Wait a second. I'm getting an idea. No. False alarm. No. Yeah. No. Yeah. No. Wait. No. Yes!
jokes.punchline: Extended indecisive internal monologue about having an idea
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment showing his scattered thinking process. A bit long but very character-specific.

jokes.text: I never want to see another can of Slurm again. I'm thirsty!
jokes.setup: Fry declaring he's done with Slurm
jokes.punchline: Immediately wanting to drink more
jokes.explanation: Perfect addiction joke showing Fry's lack of self-control. Great economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: I'm Glurmo, your guide to the splendtacular Slurm factory.
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'splendtacular' combining spectacular and splendid
jokes.explanation: Decent corporate speak parody with portmanteau word creation.

jokes.text: Can we have our free Slurm? - You'll have all you can drink...when you're partying with my good friend, Slurms McKenzie.
jokes.setup: Direct request for promised free product
jokes.punchline: Deflection with vague future promise
jokes.explanation: Standard corporate deflection humor, functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: He better be. That's why we pay him, right? / Right. / Slurms has to party all night every night or he's fired.
jokes.setup: Asking if party mascot is ready to party
jokes.punchline: Revealing partying is forced labor with threat of termination
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark subversion of party mascot concept. Shows corporate exploitation through cheerful delivery.

jokes.text: He better be. That's why we pay him, right? - Right. Slurms has to party all night every night or he's fired.
jokes.setup: Expecting party mascot to be enthusiastic
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's forced labor, not genuine enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Dark revelation about mascot exploitation, subverts party atmosphere

jokes.text: I'm gonna go lie down.
jokes.setup: Party mascot before big party event
jokes.punchline: Exhausted party animal wanting to rest instead of partying
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of party animal stereotype. Shows the tragic reality behind the persona.

jokes.text: the wondrous world of whimsy that we call... Slurm Centralized Industrial Fabrication Unit.
jokes.punchline: Boring corporate industrial name
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and parody of corporate doublespeak versus fantasy factory naming.

jokes.text: Those are the Grunka Lunkas! / Tell them I hate them.
jokes.punchline: Bender's immediate unprovoked hostility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - immediate hostility toward innocent workers. Good Wonka reference.

jokes.text: What's the secret ingredient? / Whatever you want it to be! / But what is it really? / That's not for you to know.
jokes.punchline: Evasive non-answer followed by direct refusal
jokes.explanation: Good corporate evasion parody but fairly standard mysterious ingredient setup.

jokes.text: using sensual massage
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately sexual method for food production
jokes.explanation: Absurd manufacturing process, mild sexual humor

jokes.text: Grunka Lunka dunkity doo / We've got a friendly warning for you / Grunka Lunka dunkity dasis / The secret of Slurm's On a need-to-know basis
jokes.punchline: Corporate secrecy message instead of moral lesson
jokes.explanation: Excellent parody that subverts the wholesome Oompa Loompa songs with corporate intimidation. Great structure and memorable.

jokes.text: I don't pay you to sing. You just used your bathroom break. / Hard ass. / I heard that.
jokes.punchline: Treating song as bathroom break, worker's insult, boss hearing it
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing corporate dystopia through bathroom break policy and worker relations.

jokes.text: Grunka Lunka dunkity dinkredient / You should not ask About secret ingredient / Okay, we get the point. / I was curious about the guards. / Grunka Lunka dunkity darmed guards- / Shut up.
jokes.setup: Grunka Lunkas singing another warning song
jokes.punchline: Trying to warn about everything, getting cut off
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the running gag. Shows they have warnings for everything but get shut down.

jokes.text: They don't have a good union. / They're like slaves.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Grunka Lunkas are cheap labor
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of slavery
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor about corporate exploitation. Perfect economy and escalation from bad union to slavery.

jokes.text: No. That's moronic. - Fine. I'll swim around and drink as much as I want.
jokes.punchline: Fry proceeding with even worse plan
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's stubborn addiction overriding logic

jokes.text: Help! I can't swim!
jokes.punchline: Realizing he can't swim after jumping in
jokes.explanation: Shows Fry's impulsiveness and poor planning. Predictable physical comedy setup.

jokes.text: Everybody was doing it. I just wanted to be popular.
jokes.punchline: Admitting peer pressure motivation
jokes.explanation: Good subversion showing Bender's insecurity despite his tough exterior.

jokes.text: My God, what if the secret ingredient is people? / There's already a soda like that. Soylent Cola. / How is it? / It varies from person to person.
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to people-based soda and taste variation pun
jokes.explanation: Brilliant layered joke combining Soylent Green reference with perfect pun on taste variation. Excellent writing.

jokes.text: There's already a soda like that. Soylent Cola. - How is it? - It varies from person to person.
jokes.punchline: Taste varies based on source person
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark pun, perfect wordplay on 'varies from person to person' - one of the episode's best jokes

jokes.text: Weren't there more people at the start of the tour?
jokes.punchline: Implication they've been eliminated
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor building tension about factory's sinister nature

jokes.text: I'm never going 12 minutes without a Slurm again.
jokes.punchline: Specific short time period showing severity of addiction
jokes.explanation: Good specificity showing the absurd level of Fry's addiction with precise time measurement.

jokes.text: I'm never going 12 minutes without a Slurm again.
jokes.setup: Fry finding Slurm after brief absence
jokes.punchline: Extremely short timeframe showing severe addiction
jokes.explanation: Shows extreme addiction with specific timeframe, good character consistency

jokes.text: Still warm.
jokes.setup: Fry drinking fresh Slurm from source
jokes.punchline: Noting the disturbing warmth of bodily secretion
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment with excellent economy. The implication is deeply disturbing but delivered casually.

jokes.text: That's the secret ingredient? / That's the only ingredient of Slurm.
jokes.punchline: It's not just an ingredient, it's the entire product
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that makes the revelation even more disgusting. Great structure and economy.

jokes.text: Honey comes from a bee's behind. Milk is from a cow's behind. Do you use toothpaste? / Whose behind is that? / I won't say.
jokes.setup: Defending bodily secretion products with examples
jokes.punchline: Toothpaste mystery ingredient reveal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that makes the audience reconsider everyday products. Excellent structure and disturbing implications.

jokes.text: Yes! Which is why we'll market it as New Slurm. / When everyone hates it... we'll bring back Slurm Classic and make billions.
jokes.setup: Poor quality Slurm from human queen
jokes.punchline: Reference to Coca-Cola's New Coke marketing disaster
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to real marketing history. Shows corporate cynicism and manipulation perfectly.

jokes.text: It's so delicious, you'll eat until you explode. / Which reminds me, put a tarp over that sofa.
jokes.punchline: Casual preparation for victim's explosive death
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with practical consideration of messy death. Great economy and structure.

jokes.text: You pig! Stop stuffing your craw and save us!
jokes.setup: Fry distracted by food during rescue
jokes.punchline: Leela's exasperated insult using bird anatomy term
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leela's frustration. 'Craw' is a nice specific insult choice.

jokes.text: I could fit if I didn't have these damn arms.
jokes.setup: Trying to escape through small opening
jokes.punchline: Blaming arms instead of his large torso
jokes.explanation: Good Bender logic - blaming the wrong body part. Shows his self-deception tendencies.

jokes.text: I'm partied out. All I want is to stay home and rent videos. / Is that so much to ask?
jokes.punchline: Wanting boring domestic life instead of partying
jokes.explanation: Perfect character subversion showing the tragic reality behind the party persona. Touching and funny.

jokes.text: But she'll crush you like a worm. A smaller worm. / I'm so tired of partying. So very tired.
jokes.punchline: Slurms choosing death over more partying
jokes.explanation: Touching character moment showing Slurms' exhaustion. Good 'smaller worm' detail from Leela.

jokes.text: I'll save you the only way I know how. By partying!
jokes.punchline: Sacrifice involves doing what he's tired of doing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant heroic sacrifice that's both noble and tragic. Perfect character consistency - saving others through his skill.

jokes.text: Party on, Slurms. Party on, contest winners. Party on.
jokes.setup: Final words of dying party mascot
jokes.punchline: Last words are party encouragement
jokes.explanation: Perfect final moment staying true to character while being genuinely touching. Great callback reference.

jokes.text: Grandpa's making up stories again. / I'm not your grandpa. You're my uncle from the year 2000. / Okay, Grandpa.
jokes.punchline: Time travel relationship confusion dismissed as senility
jokes.explanation: Good time travel comedy showing how impossible it is to explain these relationships to others.

jokes.text: Hey, that's not that bad.
jokes.setup: Tasting regular Slurm after knowing the truth
jokes.punchline: Still enjoying it despite knowing it's worm secretion
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending showing Fry's addiction overcoming his disgust. Great character consistency and disturbing implication.
